title: Google Calendar
content:
Google Calendar is our primary tool for scheduling meetings and events. You can access the app via the G Suite SSO Calendar tile in your Okta Dashboard or by directly going to the Calendar page .

This page aims to provide solutions for some of the most commonly asked questions we have received. For more in-depth support, please visit the official Calendar support page .

Calendar Access Requests

Create a Calendar Event

Create Out of Office Events

Access Permissions for Events

Set Up Calendar Delegation

Subscribe to Calendars

Calendar Access Requests

You can use this AR template to request the following:

Deletion of a calendar event

Access to someone’s calendar

Create a Calendar Event

On the top-left of the screen, click on the + Create button, followed by Event Alternatively you can also click directly on the time-slot where you’d like to schedule the event

Alternatively you can also click directly on the time-slot where you’d like to schedule the event

Customize the event by specifying a title, adding guests, and selecting which video conferencing option you’d like to use Please see our Google Calendar Zoom extension guide for more details

Please see our Google Calendar Zoom extension guide for more details

Create Out of Office Events

On the top-left of the screen, click on the + Create button, followed by Out of office

Specify the timeframe for the Out of office event

Specify how you would like to handle meeting invites while you are out of office

Access Permissions for Events

Open the Settings by clicking on the gear icon near the top-right of the screen, followed by Settings

On the left-hand-side, under Settings for my calendars select the calendar you want to modify the visibility settings for

Click on Calendar settings followed by + Add people and groups

Under Access permissions for events you can modify how people inside of GitLab and external partners can view your events

It is not possible to share a detailed view of your primary calendar's events with people outside of GitLab (i.e no @gitlab.com email address)

Set Up Calendar Delegation

Open the Settings by clicking on the gear icon near the top-right of the screen, followed by Settings

On the left-hand-side, under Settings for my calendars select the calendar you want to delegate access to

Click on Shared with followed by + Add people and groups

Select the people our groups you would like to add as delegates and the permission level they should have

Subscribe to Calendars

Locate the Other calendars section on the left of the screen

Click the + button and select Subscribe to calendars

Enter the email address of the user or group whose calendar you’d like to subscribe to
